summary: Tested requested history storage/display end-to-end on public URL. New dashboard chat exchanges persist to lightweight in-memory history, /api/history returns combined records, and dashboard recent assessments updates immediately after chat submit.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout is missing; attempts 1-6 with wrong password all returned 401 with no lockout/429 behavior.
No app-owned auth httpOnly session cookie is set on login (only Cloudflare __cf_bm cookie observed).

minor.issue: CORS preflight returns access-control-allow-origin: * while credentials are enabled; explicit origin is recommended for credentialed flows.
No seed_admin update path found for updating existing admin password when changed.

action_items: Implement brute-force protection/lockout after repeated failed auth attempts.
If playbook requires cookie auth, set an application auth httpOnly cookie on login.
Align CORS for credentialed requests to explicit allowlisted origins at app/edge.
Add seed_admin idempotent update behavior when admin password rotates.
critical_code_review_comments: Requested feature implementation is correct: /api/analyze-symptoms inserts per-user in-memory records and /api/history combines in-memory + persisted assessments.
DashboardPage correctly sources recent assessments from /history and re-fetches history after sending dashboard chat.
chat_history_store is in-memory only and process-local; expected for lightweight scope but records will reset on restart/scale-out.
seed_data_creation: No seeded fixture data created; generated TEST_ prefixed runtime messages only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: History feature regression added in /app/backend/tests/test_history_chat_storage.py and currently passing against public environment. Keep auth playbook hardening checks tracked separately.
rca of the issue: Auth hardening gaps are due to current JWT-in-body stateless login design: no failed-attempt persistence => no lockout trigger; no app session-cookie issuance => httpOnly requirement unmet; and edge/app CORS policy currently returns wildcard origin with credentials for preflight/login responses.
